general.title: PlanMyExams
general.description: Plan your exam schedule and discover the strongest combinations in seconds.

error.notFound: The page you are looking for does not exist

planner.heroDescription: Effortlessly plan your exam schedule and find the most optimal dates in seconds
planner.emptyStateAddByPastingHint: You can also use an external AI chatbot to convert an image to this text format.
planner.bulkPastePlaceholder: Insert your exams here using the required format...
planner.bulkSyntaxAccordionBody: This is an example of the required format:
planner.bulkSyntaxExample:
Exam: Physics 1
Dates:
- 14/06/2026
- 21/06/2026

Exam: Calculus
Dates:
- 18/06/2026
planner.bulkAiAccordionBody: Copy the prompt below, paste it into your AI chatbot, attach an image with your exam list, then paste the generated plain text result here.
planner.bulkAiPromptTemplate:
I will send you an image containing exam names and possible dates.
Extract all exams and return ONLY plain text in this exact format:

Exam: <EXAM NAME>
Dates:
- dd/mm/yyyy
- dd/mm/yyyy

Exam: <EXAM NAME>
Dates:
- dd/mm/yyyy

Rules:
- Keep one block per exam.
- Separate different exam blocks with exactly one empty line.
- Use date format dd/mm/yyyy.
- Keep the exact labels 'Exam:' and 'Dates:' (case-sensitive).
- Every date line must start with '- '.
- Do NOT use markdown code fences (```), bullets outside date lines, or any extra text before/after the blocks.
planner.sameTimeQuestion: How many exams do you prepare for at the same time?
planner.calculateErrorSameTime: The number of exams prepared at the same time must be less than the total number of exams
planner.outputDescriptionPrefix: These combinations have been evaluated considering the preparation of
planner.outputScoreHint: Each combination has a score in the top right corner showing how optimal it is.
planner.modalErrorMissingDates: Please fill in all date fields
planner.modalErrorDuplicateName: An exam with this name already exists: please use a different name
planner.modalErrorBulkEmpty: Paste your exam text before importing
planner.modalErrorBulkSyntax: Invalid format. Use: Exam: <name>, Dates:, and one or more lines with - dd/mm/yyyy
